title: Annual Program Review Update 2024 Business 12-16-24 (Part 1)
text:
Annual Program Review Update
Fall 2024
Business
1. Describe annual progress/changes/events in the program
Plans to create an Entrepreneurship Certificate are moving forward. The Curriculum
Committee approved the revisions to BA 1016 Introduction to Entrepreneurship (formerly
BA 0985), increasing it to 3 units and making it CSU transferable. The course can now be
added to the proposed Entrepreneurship Certificate program in eLumen for a proposal start
date of Fall 2024.
a. Additional information is needed before the Curriculum Committee can move
forward to approve the program. Therefore, the proposal start date will be changed
to Fall 2025.
b. BA 1016 Introduction to Entrepreneurship was approved by the Chancellor’s Office
March 8, 2024. It is CSU transferable and may be offered as a single course prior to
the approval of the Entrepreneurship Certificate. The suggested start date to offer
the course is Summer 2025 or late-start Fall 2025.
The Curriculum Committee approved revisions to BA 1004 Business Communication to align
with C-ID BUS 115, 3 Units and CSU transferable. The committee also agreed to un-crosslist
BA 1004 Business Communication from COMS 1004 and resubmit for C-ID approval. Once
the course receives C-ID approval, it will be added to the Business Administration 2.0 AS-T
(which was approved in Fall 2022). In addition, the course may be added to the Business
and Industry Professional (BIP) Certificate in eLumen with a proposal start date of Fall 2024.
a. BA 1004 Business Communication revisions were made and updated in eLumen to
reflect that it is CSU transferrable. Upon confirmation from the Chancellor’s Office
that the course is CI-D approved, the suggested start date to offer the course is Fall
2025.
Shasta College is reviewing the courses required in the COS Accounting degree and
Certificate programs for a proposed Accounting Pre-Apprenticeship that would align with
Shasta College’s Apprenticeship program. If approved, COS students that meet the
requirements of the Pre-Apprenticeship will be automatically accepted into Shasta College’s
Accounting Apprenticeship.

-- 1 of 3 --

title: Annual Program Review Update 2024 Business 12-16-24 (Part 2)
text:
2. Describe progress on assessing SLOs and PLOs for the program
The Business program submitted its SLO assessment schedule for 2023-2026. Based on the
schedule, 7131 BA 1006 Introduction to Business was assessed in Fall 2023 and 3056 BA
1018 Legal Environment of Business was assessed in Spring 2024.
3. Discuss any support or obstacles encountered by the program
Funding in the amount of $2,480.00 (5 hours per week for 16 weeks) was approved for a
student worker to help grow the proposed entrepreneurship pathway. This worker will
conduct research and support the community outreach and marketing activities as well
as serve as an ambassador to bring awareness to the program, beginning Spring 2024.
a. The student worker was going to be funded through The Learning Aligned
Employment Program (LAEP). However, the program was terminated prior to the
student worker being hired.
4. Describe program budgetary needs or implications (Submit a CQIP if you
are requesting a budget allocation that falls outside of regular yearly
budget allocations).
Approximately $10,000 of ongoing funds are needed to attend the following
conferences and to support student trips:
a. National Association for Community College Entrepreneurship (NACCE) annual
conference held in October. The annual membership of $900 is paid through
Noncredit funds. NACCE is a network of entrepreneurial leaders and educators. The
organization provides funding to develop entrepreneurship education programs,
seed money for launching entrepreneurial ventures, and student pitch contests. It is
a national organization that represents 300+ community and technical colleges.
Approximately $3,500
$5,000 may be recouped by applying for a grant through NACCE to host a
business pitch competition for students
b. California Community Colleges Association of Occupational Education (CCCAOE),
April 8-11, 2025, Sacramento, CA – Approximately $2,000
c. GrowTECH Fest (an entrepreneurial ecosystem in the North State), October or
November 2025, Chico, CA – Approximately $1,000
d. The Rural Summit: From Cradle to Career (The Rural Summit brings together
dedicated rural leaders who share a commitment to advancing the futures of rural
children, families and communities. It is attended by educators, community leaders,

-- 2 of 3 --

title: Annual Program Review Update 2024 Business 12-16-24 (Part 3)
text:
higher education leaders, elected officials, workforce professionals, philanthropic
leaders and rural residents who exchange innovative ideas and effective strategies
to ensure that every rural youth is on the path to upward mobility from birth to
career), April 27-30, 2025, Anchorage, Alaska – Approximately $3,500
e. Funds are needed for transportation, i.e. van and driver for student trips, including
conferences, networking events, or other professional development activities
throughout Butte, Shasta, and Siskiyou Counties. Cost estimates are needed for the
use of COS vans and drivers.
5. Closing the loop: In the last year, were any of your program CQIPs
approved? If so, how did this additional budget allocation improve or
support your program?
November 2022, IPB approved the recommendation of the Faculty Prioritization
Taskforce to hire an additional full-time Business faculty. Following a failed search and
other concerns regarding student enrollments, the Department Dean and business
faculty agreed to postpone hiring additional full-time faculty for at least one year or
until enrollments increase and more classes are offered.
To follow-up, enrollments have not increased enough to warrant an additional
full-time faculty member. The courses required for the degrees and certificates
in the Business program are offered on a regular rotation. Additional faculty
needs may be addressed, as new programs and courses are offered.

-- 3 of 3 --
